ABRAMOV'S ALLEGATIONS OF FACTS NOT SET FORTH IN THE COMPLAINT, AND HE IS INCORRECT IN ARGUING THAT PLAINTIFF MUST PRODUCE EVIDENCE OR DOCUMENTS AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE. PLAINTIFF IS ALLOWED TO PLEAD FACTS ON THE BASIS OF INFORMATION AND BELIEF. THAT SAID, HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (WHICH MR. ABRAMOV SHOULD HAVE SUBMITTED WITH HIS MOVING PAPERS, SFLR 2.6(C)) BOIL DOWN TO A LEGAL CONCLUSION. IT IS NOT SUFFICIENT THAT DEFENDANT IS AN OFFICER OR MANAGING MEMBER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Edward Alkhasov'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff
Off Calendar Feb-19-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: EDWARD ALKASOV, 415.869.1954, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Edward Alkhasov's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff is Granted, in part. The deposition of Plaintiff shall take place on March 20, 2013 at 10:00 a.m. at a court reporter's office in the City and County of San Francisco to be selected by Mr. Alkhasov. The selected reporter must be a certified court reporter. Mr. Alkhasov must provide a certified court interpreter in English/Spanish. The interpreter must bring evidence of certification to the deposition for review by Plaintiff's counsel. The deposition notice shall not include any other document request, but merely the location of the deposition. Mr. Alkhasov must serve notice of the deposition to all parties by mail no later than March 5, 2013. Plaintiff need not produce her government issued identification or passport. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-15-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: VLADIMIR ABRAMOV, 415.786.2119, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.
EDWARD ALKHASOV, 415.869.1954, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: no argument. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT VLADIMIR ABRAMOV'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with 10 days leave to amend to plead facts supporting Plaintiff's alter ego allegation. The Court disregards all of Mr. Abramov's allegations of facts not set forth in the complaint, and he is incorrect in arguing that Plaintiff must produce evidence or documents at this stage of the case. Plaintiff is allowed to plead facts on the basis of information and belief. That said, however, Plaintiff's alter ego allegations in the first amended complaint (which Mr. Abramov should have submitted with his moving papers, SFLR 2.6(C)) boil down to a legal conclusion. It is not sufficient that defendant is an officer or managing member. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
